title: Culture of Established Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell Lines
author: Cellular Generation and Phenotyping
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgbwjspe

steps.title: Preparation Coat a 6 well plate...
steps.instruction: Preparation Coat a 6 well plate with vitronectin and incubate according to the manufacturer's instructions. Prepare complete E8 or TeSR-E8 media according to the manufacturer's instructions.

steps.title: Prepare thawing media by supplementing culture...
steps.instruction: Prepare thawing media by supplementing culture media with rock inhibitor (Y-27632) to a final concentration of 10μM.

steps.title: Add 8ml of thawing media per...
steps.instruction: Add 8ml of thawing media per cryovial being thawed into a sterile 15ml falcon tube(s).

steps.title: Partially thaw the frozen cryovial(s) of...
steps.instruction: Partially thaw the frozen cryovial(s) of iPS cells in a 37 °C water bath until there is a small ice crystal remaining.

steps.title: Add 1ml of thawing media dropwise to each cryovial
steps.instruction: Add 1ml of thawing media dropwise to each cryovial. Collect the full volume of media and cell suspension and add to the Falcon tube prepared in step 3. iPSCs should be kept as small clumps as much as possible to increase survival efficiency; minimize the amount of pipetting when thawing cells to reduce the number of single cells in the cell suspension.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 120 rcf for 3 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 120 rcf for 3 min at room temperature. 120 x g, Room temperature, 3 minutes

steps.title: Aspirate the vitronectin from the labware...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the vitronectin from the labware surface and replace with 1ml of thawing media per well. Do not allow surfaces to dry out.

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant and gently re-suspend...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant and gently re-suspend each cell pellet in 1ml of thawing media. Plate the cell suspension into the 1ml of thawing media in the well(s).

steps.title: Agitate the plate gently within a...
steps.instruction: Agitate the plate gently within a tissue culture incubator set at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 to ensure even distribution of cells across the well.

steps.title: The cells will take 2-24 hours...
steps.instruction: The cells will take 2-24 hours to attach to the surface. Make sure the plate is not disturbed during this time. 24 hours after plating, do a full media change to remove the rock inhibitor.

steps.title: Preparation Coat a 6 well plate(s)...
steps.instruction: Preparation Coat a 6 well plate(s) with vitronectin and incubate according to the manufacturer's instructions.

steps.title: Media change the cells every 24...
steps.instruction: Media change the cells every 24 hours with culture media until they reach 70-80% confluency; at this point they need to be passaged.

steps.title: Prepare fresh 0
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 0.5mM EDTA by diluting UltraPure 0.5M EDTA, pH 8.0 with DPBS(-/-). Store at room temperature and use on day of preparation only.

steps.title: Aspirate the spent medium from cells...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the spent medium from cells and wash with 2ml of DPBS(-/-) per well.

steps.title: Aspirate DPBS(-/-) and add 1-2ml of 0
steps.instruction: Aspirate DPBS(-/-) and add 1-2ml of 0.5mM EDTA per well. Rock the dishes to cover the surface of the cells and incubate for 4 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Observe the colonies under a microscope...
steps.instruction: Observe the colonies under a microscope until colonies display shiny ‘halos’ around the edges and holes appear throughout the colonies.

steps.title: Aspirate the EDTA solution and add...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the EDTA solution and add 2ml of culture medium per well.

steps.title: Collect and dispense medium across the...
steps.instruction: Collect and dispense medium across the labware surface up to 3 times to detach cells. If colonies are still attached, repeat wash with same volume of fresh media. Avoid creating bubbles. Collect the cells in a falcon tube.

steps.title: Aspirate victronectin from new labware surfaces...
steps.instruction: Aspirate victronectin from new labware surfaces and replace with 1ml of pre-warmed culture media. Do not allow surfaces to dry out.

steps.title: Add required amount of culture medium...
steps.instruction: Add required amount of culture medium to cell suspension depending on desired split ratio. Aim for a split ratio where the cells are passaged approximately every 4-5 days. The split ratio may have to be adjusted depending on cell quality.

steps.title: Plate 1ml of cell suspension into each new well
steps.instruction: Plate 1ml of cell suspension into each new well.

steps.title: Agitate the plate gently within a...
steps.instruction: Agitate the plate gently within a tissue culture incubator set at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 to ensure even distribution of cells across the well.

steps.title: When colonies are compact and roughly...
steps.instruction: When colonies are compact and roughly 70% confluent they are ready to be frozen. One confluent well of a 6 well plate should be frozen down into 5 cryovials.

steps.title: The cells will take 2-24 hours...
steps.instruction: The cells will take 2-24 hours to attach to the surface. Media change the cells every 24 hours. Culture the cells until they reach 70-80% confluency; at this point they can passaged again for further expansion.

steps.title: Make fresh freezing media using 10%...
steps.instruction: Make fresh freezing media using 10% DMSO in KSR (for example 1mL DMSO in 9mL KSR) and store at 4°C until required.

steps.title: Aspirate the spent medium from the...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the spent medium from the labware and wash with 2ml of DPBS(-/-) per well.

steps.title: Aspirate the DPBS(-/-) and add 2ml of 0
steps.instruction: Aspirate the DPBS(-/-) and add 2ml of 0.5mM EDTA per well.

steps.title: Rock the dishes to cover the...
steps.instruction: Rock the dishes to cover the surface of the cells and incubate for 4-8 min at room temperature. Observe the colonies under a microscope until colonies display shiny ‘halos’ around the edges and holes appear throughout the colonies.

steps.title: Aspirate the EDTA solution and add...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the EDTA solution and add 2ml of culture medium per well for cell collection.

steps.title: Remove the cells by gently washing...
steps.instruction: Remove the cells by gently washing the labware surface with the media up to 3 times. If >50% of colonies are still attached, repeat wash with same volume of fresh media. Avoid creating bubbles. Collect the cells into an appropriate Falcon tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the Falcon tube at 120rcf for 1 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the Falcon tube at 120rcf for 1 min. 120 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant and re-suspend pellet...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant and re-suspend pellet in enough of the freezing media (prepared in step 3) to give a total volume of 1mL per cryovial.

steps.title: Dispense 1mL of cell suspension into each cryovial
steps.instruction: Dispense 1mL of cell suspension into each cryovial. Place the vials in a CoolCell or similar freezing container and store at -80°C. Note: Once in freezing media, cells should be transferred to the -80°C freezer as quickly as possible to prevent cell death.

steps.title: After 24-48 hours at -80°C, transfer...
steps.instruction: After 24-48 hours at -80°C, transfer vials from the CoolCell into Liquid Nitrogen storage. The vials can be stored in liquid nitrogen indefinitely.

steps.title: Prepare fresh 0
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 0.5mM EDTA by diluting UltraPure 0.5M EDTA, pH 8.0 with DPBS(-/-). Store at room temperature and use on day of preparation only.
